channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Celebrity video site Cameo is now open to all creators
item.description: When the celebrity personalized video platform Cameo first launched in 2017, not just anyone could sell custom videos to fans on the platform. Cameo staff recruited some celebs to the platforms, and existing Cameo video creators were allowed to invite others they thought were successful.
item.dc:creator: Steven Melendez, Fast Company

item.title: Agentic AI: Why this emerging technology will revolutionise multiple sectors
item.description: New advancements in GenAI technology are set to create more transformative opportunities for tech-savvy enterprises and organisations. These developments come as data shows that while the GenAI boom is real and optimism is high, not every organisation is generating tangible value so far.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Adapting to Change: The Importance of Modernising Applications in Today's Business World
item.description: The rapid pace of change in the business world requires companies to adapt and modernise their applications to stay ahead. This article explores the importance of data, applications, and AI in facilitating this transformation.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Microsoft plans to make searching in Windows 11 better - I just hope it doesn't screw it up
item.description: Microsoft appears to be developing a new File Search companion app to improve Windows Search in the hopes of more accurate and helpful results.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: China bans exports to US of gallium, germanium, antimony in response to chip sanctions
item.description: BANGKOK (AP) — China announced Tuesday it is banning exports to the United States of gallium, germanium, antimony and other key high-tech materials with potential military applications, as a general principle, lashing back at U.S. limits on semiconductor-related exports.
item.dc:creator: ELAINE KURTENBACH, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Abanca emplea la IA generativa para atender las consultas de clientes y usuarios
item.description: Abanca presume de haberse consolidado como la primera entidad financiera española en integrar la inteligencia artificial (IA) generativa en la atención de las consultas de sus clientes y usuarios en general.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Understanding unstructured data in the context of AI
item.description: The volume of data being created today is truly staggering. IDC projects that global data will reach 400 zettabytes (400 billion terabytes) by 2028, with 90% of that data classified as unstructured data.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: US expands list of Chinese technology companies under export controls
item.description: The U.S. Commerce Department has expanded the list of Chinese technology companies subject to export controls to include many that make equipment used to make computer chips.
item.dc:creator: Elaine Kurtenbach, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Rust 1.83 expands const capabilities
item.description: The Rust Team has released Rust 1.83, an update to the memory-safe and thread-safe programming language that brings expanded capabilities for code running in const contexts.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: AI chatbots can make your Cyber Monday shopping a breeze, but they’re not foolproof
item.description: Tired of thinking about what gifts to get everyone this year? Artificial intelligence chatbots might help, but don’t expect them to do all the work or always give you the right answers.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fast Company

item.title: Delaware judge reaffirms ruling that invalidated massive Tesla pay package for Elon Musk
item.description: A Delaware judge has reaffirmed her ruling that Tesla must revoke Elon Musk’s multibillion-dollar pay package.
item.dc:creator: Randall Chase, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: FTC opens Microsoft antitrust investigation that Trump administration must carry on or drop
item.description: Antitrust enforcers with the Federal Trade Commission have opened a wide-ranging investigation into Microsoft's business practices, starting a big legal project that an incoming Trump administration must take up or abandon.
item.dc:creator: MATT O'BRIEN, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Here’s how two European search upstarts aim to go after Google—from the index up
item.description: Google’s dominance of web search—consistently above 85% of the market in the U.S.—is an unmissable fact of online life.
item.dc:creator: Rob Pegoraro, Fast Company

item.title: Red Hat Ansible service comes to AWS Marketplace
item.description: Red Hat Ansible Automation Platform Service, for automating the management of hybrid cloud infrastructure, now is available as a managed offering on Amazon Web Services through the AWS Marketplace. General availability of Red Hat’s automation service was announced December 2.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: The Rise of AI-Powered Publishing: A Threat or Opportunity for Traditional Book Publishers?
item.description: The growing use of artificial intelligence in publishing is changing the book market, threatening traditional publishers' business models and forcing them to adapt.
item.dc:creator: Henry Chandonnet, Fast Company

item.title: Panasonic revives its late founder as AI
item.description: Japanese electronics company Panasonic has created an AI version of its founder, Kōnosuke Matsushita, who died in 1989, The Japan Times reports.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: As temperatures turn frigid, Minnesotans turn to saunas for warmth and community
item.description: As temperatures drop into the teens, Minnesotans have embraced sauna culture for warmth and community.
item.dc:creator: Michael Goldberg And Mark Vancleave, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Bend the web to your will with this page-transforming power tool
item.description: Modern web pages aren’t exactly what you’d call “minimal.” All text aside, they’re almost always overflowing with images, ads, and other extraneous elements.
item.dc:creator: Chris Hoffman, Fast Company

item.title: No tennis partner? No worries with this AI robot
item.description: PongBot is an artificial intelligence-powered tennis robot and a smart training partner available to play 24 hours a day, seven days a week.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Bitcoin flirts with $100K as crypto industry rallies for Trump
item.description: Bitcoin has blasted through a series of record highs since President-elect Trump’s election victory as the crypto industry anticipates a significant shift in federal policy.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: What is Bluesky, the 'up-and-coming' social platform seen as X alternative
item.description: Bluesky, a new social platform garnering millions of users, is now competing with top networks like X and Truth Social. Jack Dorsey, a former CEO at Twitter, created the site in 2019 to be “a protocol for public conversation.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: Australia bans social media for children under-16
item.description: A social media ban for children under 16 passed the Australian Parliament on Friday in a world-first law.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fast Company

item.title: Canadian news publishers sue OpenAI over alleged copyright infringement
item.description: OTTAWA, Ontario (AP) — A coalition of Canadian news publishers, including The Canadian Press, Torstar, Globe and Mail, Postmedia and CBC/Radio-Canada, has filed a lawsuit against OpenAI for using news content to train its ChatGPT generative artificial intelligence system.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Amazon worker strike: 5 things to know
item.description: Thousands of Amazon workers around the world went on strike Friday, demanding more pay and better working conditions during one of the retail giant's busiest weekends of the year.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: How I turned my blog into a social media hub
item.description: A few months ago, I decided to start blogging again. Even with steady freelance work and a couple of tech advice newsletters, I missed having my own little place on the web to write about any topic I pleased, free from the limits and vagaries of social media.
item.dc:creator: Jared Newman, Fast Company

item.title: Australian Parliament bans social media for under-16s with world-first law
item.description: MELBOURNE, Australia (AP) — A social media ban for children under 16 passed the Australian Parliament on Friday in a world-first law.
item.dc:creator: ROD McGUIRK, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: 10 steps to smarter Google account security
item.description: There are important accounts to secure, and then there are important accounts to secure. Your Google account falls into that second category, maybe even with a couple of asterisks and some neon orange highlighting added in for good measure.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Copilot Studio Agents get a major upgrade via Microsoft 365 Copilot
item.description: Microsoft Ignite 2024 put an end to Microsoft’s dalliance with chatbots. Not because the company is walking away from natural language interfaces, but because it’s taking what it learned with its first Copilots and refocusing on tangible productivity gains from large language models (LLMs).
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Australian Senate debates social media ban for under-16s
item.description: The major parties' support for the ban all but guarantees the legislation will become law. But many child welfare and mental health advocates are concerned about unintended consequences.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, The Mercury News

item.title: Australia's social media ban for kids is closer to becoming law
item.description: MELBOURNE, Australia (AP) — Australia’s House of Representatives on Wednesday passed a bill that would ban children younger than 16 years old from social media, leaving it to the Senate to finalize the world-first law.
item.dc:creator: ROD McGUIRK, Associated Press, KRON4
